important.title: Do not touch services managed by the cluster
important.para:
TODO When managing a resource via the &ha; software, the resource must not be started or stopped otherwise (outside the cluster, for example manually or on boot or reboot). The &ha; software is responsible for all service start or stop actions. For interventions in resources that are currently managed by the cluster, set the resource to maintenance mode first. When managing a resource with SUSE Linux Enterprise High Availability, the same resource must not be started or stopped otherwise (outside of the cluster, for example manually or on boot or reboot). The High Availability software is responsible for all service start or stop actions.
If you need to execute testing or maintenance tasks after the services are already running under cluster control, make sure to put the resources, nodes, or the whole cluster into maintenance mode before you touch any of them manually.
